corridors.label: USD → EUR
corridors.description: The flagship transatlantic FX lane on XRPL. Direct USD↔EUR books inside Bitstamp clear the deepest fiat pair on the ledger.
corridors.useCase: Transatlantic corporate payables, Bitstamp ↔ GateHub treasury rebalancing.
highlights: USD.Bitstamp ↔ EUR.Bitstamp direct book 20/20 — arb-tight both ways
9 candidate routes give the picker counterparty diversification

corridors.label: USD → GBP
corridors.description: USD → GBP via the dedicated GateHub GBP issuer (the only GBP issuer with real bid liquidity on the ledger).
corridors.useCase: US-UK corporate settlement, GBP treasury access.
highlights: GBP.GateHub XRP legs 20/15 — deep both sides
Bitstamp GBP has near-zero sell side

corridors.label: USD → JPY
corridors.description: USD → JPY bridged via XRP into Mr. Exchange (the deepest JPY issuer on XRPL with 841M JPY outstanding).
corridors.useCase: Corporate cross-border JPY payables, SBI-adjacent flows.
highlights: JPY.Tokyo gives a second issuer for failover
Bitstamp JPY also available as a tertiary route

corridors.label: USD → CNY
corridors.description: The only major Asian fiat lane with a real direct orderbook. Three CNY issuers (RippleFox, RippleCN, RippleQK) give the picker counterparty diversification.
corridors.useCase: Greater China settlement, corporate CNY procurement, SBI Asia rails.
highlights: Three CNY issuers — automatic failover if one drops

corridors.label: USD → CHF
corridors.description: Swiss Franc lane — informational only. Single Bitstamp issuer with shallow book.
corridors.useCase: Documentation of dead lane (SIX/SARON dominates Swiss FX).
highlights: ⚠ Only 1 offer on the XRP→CHF leg

corridors.label: USD → AUD
corridors.description: Australian Dollar lane — thin. Only Bitstamp issues AUD.
corridors.useCase: Small retail AU flows, alternate ODL leg.
highlights: 3 offers on XRP→AUD leg, 1 on the return

corridors.label: EUR → USD
corridors.description: The return leg out of euros into dollars. Symmetric to USD→EUR.
corridors.useCase: European corporate USD payables, EUR liquidation.

corridors.label: EUR → GBP
corridors.description: Cross-asset lane from Euro to British Pound. The multi-route picker evaluates 12 candidate issuer combinations, scanning orderbook depth, AMM reserves, and cross-book fallbacks, then selects the deepest lowest-risk option.
corridors.useCase: Cross-currency FX lane from EUR to GBP.
highlights: 12 candidate routes (4 EUR issuers × 3 GBP issuers)
Refreshed hourly, ranked by liquidity and risk

corridors.label: EUR → JPY
